chunks.text: Jennifer Chayes, EECS Professor and Dean of the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society (CDSS) at UC Berkeley, has been named the recipient of the 2025 Richard A. Tapia Achievement Award for Scientific Scholarship, Civic Science, and Diversifying Computing.
chunks.question: What award did Jennifer Chayes receive in 2025?
chunks.answer: The 2025 Richard A. Tapia Achievement Award.

chunks.text: EECS Professor Emeritus Jan Rabaey has been selected to receive the 2026 IEEE Donald O. Pederson Award in Solid-State Circuits. This prestigious award recognizes his outstanding contributions to the field of solid-state circuits, as measured by his impact on society, innovation, and leadership in the field.
chunks.question: Which award did Jan Rabaey receive for solid-state circuits?
chunks.answer: The 2026 IEEE Donald O. Pederson Award.

chunks.text: EECS Professors Alvin Cheung and Sophia Shao, and their students Charles Hong and Sahil Batia, have won the Best Paper Award at the Machine Learning for Computer Architecture and Systems (MLArchSys) Workshop, held at ISCA 2025. Their award-winning paper, titled “Autocomp: LLM-Driven Code Optimization for Tensor Accelerators”, presents a novel approach.
chunks.question: What award did EECS researchers win at MLArchSys?
chunks.answer: Best Paper Award for Autocomp paper.

chunks.text: EECS researchers Jonathan Bachrach, John Wawrzynek, Krste Asanovic, and their students and collaborators have received the Design Automation Conference’s (DAC) Most Influential Paper Award for the decade 2010–2020. Their groundbreaking paper, “Chisel: Constructing Hardware in a Scala Embedded Language” (2012), was recognized.
chunks.question: Which paper won DAC's Most Influential Paper Award?

chunks.text: EECS Assistant Professor Sagar Karandikar has been awarded the 2025 ACM SIGARCH/IEEE CS TCCA Outstanding Dissertation Award. This prestigious honor recognizes exceptional doctoral research in the field of computer architecture, specifically for advancing agile hardware/software co-design tools.
chunks.question: What award did Sagar Karandikar receive?

chunks.text: Professors Ken Goldberg, John Kubiatowicz, and their team have received the Best Robot Learning Paper Award at the IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2025. Their research involved students and collaborators including Kaiyuan Chen and Letian Fu.
chunks.question: Which award did EECS researchers win at IEEE ICRA 2025?

chunks.text: Prabal Dutta, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, has been awarded the Distinguished Alumni Award for Academic Excellence from the College of Engineering at The Ohio State University. This award honors alumni who have demonstrated significant achievement in academia.
chunks.question: What award did Prabal Dutta receive from Ohio State?
chunks.answer: Distinguished Alumni Award for Academic Excellence.

chunks.text: EECS Professor Emeritus Michael A. Harrison passed away on Saturday, June 7. He was 89. Professor Harrison earned his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Michigan in 1963 and joined UC Berkeley as an assistant professor that same year, spending most of his academic career there.
chunks.question: When did EECS Professor Michael Harrison pass away?

chunks.text: EECS Teaching Professors Dan Garcia and Brian Harvey have been awarded the Karl V. Karlstrom Outstanding Educator Award by the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM). They were recognized for their advocacy and advances in education to bring beauty and joy to computing.
chunks.question: Which educators won the ACM Karl V. Karlstrom Award?

chunks.text: EECS Professor Stuart Russell has been elected as a Fellow of the Royal Society, the UK’s national academy of sciences. He was among ninety researchers from around the world named in this year’s cohort, recognized for scientific achievements from basic discovery to impactful research.
chunks.question: What honor did Stuart Russell receive from the Royal Society?
